steps.notes: Issue and prior PR state reviewed; no new maintainer stop or direction change.

steps.description: Map shell, settings, IPC, window, and routing sources
steps.notes: Mapped shell/projector, settings persistence, Electron window/IPC, and environment-thread routing.
Canonical design, acceptance matrix, sensors, evidence, and PR boundaries recorded.

steps.description: Review design against acceptance criteria and prior PR failures
steps.notes: Reviewed prior failure modes, canonical settled state, replay generations, privacy boundary, native failure handling, close retention, and click startup race. No unresolved blocker.
Persisted default-off setting, legacy decode, desktop-only UI, and restore-default behavior implemented and tested.

decisions.title: Use canonical thread shell state
decisions.description: Derive alerts from settled latestTurn and rising pending flags in OrchestrationThreadShell, gated by environment synchronization. Provider-specific events remain outside the feature.

decisions.title: Deliver from Electron main
decisions.description: Use Electron Notification in the main process so native delivery, real BrowserWindow focus, process-lifetime deduplication, and click restoration share one policy boundary.

decisions.title: Keep notification payload generic
decisions.description: Renderer sends only event kind, stable ID, environment ID, and thread ID. Desktop owns fixed copy and silent delivery, preventing agent output, prompts, diffs, commands, titles, or project names from appearing.

decisions.title: Use pending navigation consumption
decisions.description: On click, Electron stores one pending target, restores or creates and focuses the main window, signals the renderer, and lets the renderer consume the target once. This closes the startup-listener race.

decisions.title: Retain opted-in macOS renderer on close
decisions.description: When desktop notifications are enabled on macOS and the app is not quitting, prevent main-window destruction and hide it. This preserves live shell observation while the standard macOS process remains active. Disabled settings and Quit keep existing destruction semantics.
alternatives: Duplicate environment subscriptions in Electron main
Create a separate hidden observer window
